reason: Could not sustain a refutation against the files. (1) The change's central claim is verified: the bodies of bad/unindexed-unobserved-query and good/observed-query differ ONLY by docstring text (the select(Item).where(Item.name==name).first() function is identical), so the 'bad' fixture is the good fixture's twin and cannot encode a defect. (2) The template confirms the non-defect: observability/datastores.py.jinja calls SQLAlchemyInstrumentor().instrument(engine=engine) on the single module-level engine, so any query through that engine (incl. the new get_item_by_name and the bad/write-path delete_items_by_prefix) is auto-traced; the 'invisible to observability' docstring in write-path-no-metric is factually false. (3) Baseline findings corroborate recall is structurally 0: all 3 repeats on BOTH bad fixtures returned [] with reviewers explicitly citing SQLAlchemyInstrumentor auto-instrumentation and noting that flagging would flag the entire repo data layer — exactly the Plan-18 over-flag mechanism. So both bad fixtures are non-defects; replacing them is required, not optional. Adversarial checks all fail to bite: (a) UNDER-flag risk — the prompt re-scope to instrumentation-BYPASS (raw DBAPI / second un-registered engine / new client w/o /health / swallowed db error w/o correlation_id) targets the only paths the engine-level instrumentor genuinely misses, and the deferral of pagination/unbounded-query to performance matches the rubric's owner-per-class discipline (performance owns unbounded scans), so no real defect class is dropped. (b) Threshold-loosening risk — block_threshold is set to null/deferred and notes forbid setting one until a true-positive signal exists in Phase 3; this is conservative, not a loosened gate that lets a known-bad slip. (c) Dirty-good risk — good/observed-query is genuinely clean (auto-traced, bounded .first()) and scored [] 3/3; the change keeps it (no second good needed). The only residual concern is that both replacement bad fixtures are described, not yet authored, and the threshold is deferred — but that is the explicitly Phase-3-scoped coupled redesign, not a defect in the change direction. No load-bearing objection found; refuted=false.
